title: Rootless podman pod can't start with systemd
body:
### Issue Description

I usually try to debug these issues myself but I must've come across a bug in either recent podman or systemd. 





### Steps to reproduce the issue

I've simplified the steps to reproduce this is much as possible:
$ podman pod create --name=media
$ cd ~/.config/systemd/user/
$ podman generate systemd -f -n --new media
/home/medianet/.config/systemd/user/pod-media.service
$ systemctl --user daemon-reload
$ systemctl --user start pod-media
Job for pod-media.service failed because the service did not take the steps required by its unit configuration.
See "systemctl --user status pod-media.service" and "journalctl --user -xeu pod-media.service" for details.


### Describe the results you received

When I try to start my pod I get the following in the journal:

$ journalctl --user -r
systemd[95694]: Failed to start Podman pod-media.service.
systemd[95694]: pod-media.service: Failed with result 'protocol'.
systemd[95694]: pod-media.service: New main PID 98925 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
systemd[95694]: pod-media.service: New main PID 98925 does not belong to service, and PID file is not owned by root. Refusing.
systemd[95694]: Failed to start Podman pod-media.service.

$ systemctl --user status pod-media.service
× pod-media.service - Podman pod-media.service
     Loaded: loaded (/home/medianet/.config/systemd/user/pod-media.service; disabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: failed (Result: protocol) since Fri 2023-05-12 17:35:45 EEST; 1s ago
       Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
    Process: 99618 ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/podman pod create --infra-conmon-pidfile /run/user/1001/pod-media.pid --pod-id-file /run/user/1001/pod-media.pod-id --exit-policy=stop --name=media --replace (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 99632 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman pod start --pod-id-file /run/user/1001/pod-media.pod-id (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
    Process: 99654 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman pod rm --ignore -f --pod-id-file /run/user/1001/pod-media.pod-id (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)
        CPU: 58ms

May 12 17:35:45 hawking systemd[95694]: pod-media.service: Start request repeated too quickly.
May 12 17:35:45 hawking systemd[95694]: pod-media.service: Failed with result 'protocol'.
May 12 17:35:45 hawking systemd[95694]: Failed to start Podman pod-media.service.

$ journalctl --user -xeu pod-media.service
systemd[95694]: Failed to start Podman pod-media.service.
░░ Subject: A start job for unit UNIT has failed
░░ Defined-By: systemd
░░ Support: https://lists.freedesktop.org/mailman/listinfo/systemd-devel
░░ 
░░ A start job for unit UNIT has finished with a failure.
░░ 
░░ The job identifier is 2323 and the job result is failed.


### Describe the results you expected

The pod starting up normally.

### podman info output

```yaml
$ podman info

  arch: amd64

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 4
      libseccomp: 2.5.4
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 16h 36m 58.00s (Approximately 0.67 days)

  GoVersion: go1.20.3
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.5.0


systemd 253 (253.4-1-arch)
+PAM +AUDIT -SELINUX -APPARMOR -IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS +FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN +IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 -PWQUALITY +P11KIT -QRENCODE +TPM2 +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD +BPF_FRAMEWORK +XKBCOMMON +UTMP -SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

This is a archlinux install with the latest updates. No weird settings or so other then using apparmor and lockdown=confidentiality, but I've tried to disable both of those without any luck.

### Additional information

I've tried podman system migrate, podman system reset, running without any containers.conf or storage.conf from either '$HOME/.config/containers', '/etc/containers' or '/usr/share/containers'. But no luck.

I have a raspberry running archlinuxarm with mostly the same software versions, podman 4.5.0, systemd 253 (253.3-3-arch) where this problem does NOT exist. So yeah, I've really been scratching my head the last days.

Would greatly appreciate any input or help here!

comments.body:
It's funny how after writing a bug report you finally figure out the problem.

Some weeks ago I signed up for mullvads vpn service and installed their software on this machine. And apparently the mullvad daemon mounts a net_cls cgroup which I guess confused systemd's cgroup management. Apparently mullvad does this to allow split tunnel vpn's on Linux.

Lost so many hours to this...
